rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. DEFENDANT FOSTER LUMBER YARD, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Foster Lumber Yard, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff Woody Sims does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) Defendant does not address Plaintiff's interrogatory response indicating that he purchased asbestos-containing plumbers putty at Foster Lumber in Vallejo, California. (Defendant's Ex. C at 2; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [the court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) The Court does not address whether the exposure caused Plaintiff's injury, as argued in Defendant's moving papers, as Defendant did not address causation its separate statement of undisputed material facts. (United Cmty. Church v. Garcin (1991) 231 Cal. App. 3d 327, 337 (superseded by statute on other grounds as stated in Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's of London v. Superior Court (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 952, 957, fn. 4).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE FANNIN VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 26, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. PLAINTIFF CLARENCE FANNIN's Motion For Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff Plaintiff's Motion for Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest for Deceased Plaintiff is granted. Nicholas Fannin is substituted as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff, Clarence Fannin pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 377.31. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
